name: Model and Generation Effects Search Pass

inclusion_criteria: The source compares synthetic or simulated student response behavior across LLM families, model versions, post-training methods, ensemble choices, or generation workflows.
The source reports model- or workflow-specific quality, fidelity, psychometric, or downstream utility effects for generated learner/student responses.
The source has a primary source page, proceedings page, DOI metadata, arXiv page, or full-text PDF available for verification.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude sources whose only variation is prompt wording unless the source also compares model, post-training, ensemble, decoding, or broader generation workflow effects.
Exclude generic LLM tutoring or feedback studies that do not generate or evaluate synthetic student responses.
Exclude summary pages when a primary proceedings, DOI, or arXiv source is available.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by title, DOI, arXiv identifier, venue metadata, and canonical source page.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 35 web and primary-source records across targeted queries

screening_decisions.title: Agent4Edu: Generating Learner Response Data by Generative Agents for Intelligent Education Systems
screening_decisions.reason: Reports Agent4Edu workflow modules and a bounded GPT-3.5-turbo versus GPT-4 exploratory comparison for generated learner response performance.

screening_decisions.title: Can LLMs Reliably Simulate Real Students' Abilities in Mathematics and Reading Comprehension?
screening_decisions.reason: Directly compares 11 LLMs and prompt settings against real NAEP response patterns on a shared IRT ability scale.

screening_decisions.title: Simulated Students in Tutoring Dialogues: Substance or Illusion?
screening_decisions.reason: Compares prompt-based simulated students with SFT and DPO post-training workflows across dialogue fidelity metrics.

screening_decisions.title: Leveraging LLM Respondents for Item Evaluation: A Psychometric Analysis
screening_decisions.reason: Compares six LLM respondent generators and ensemble/resampling strategies against human College Algebra response distributions and item calibration.

screening_decisions.title: Who Am I? History-Aware Profiles for Student Simulation in Tutoring Dialogues
screening_decisions.reason: Recent arXiv source comparing prompting, fine-tuning, history conditioning, profile conditioning, DPO, and GRPO workflow variants for simulated student turns.

screening_decisions.title: Calibrating Generative AI to Produce Realistic Essays for Data Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to prompt-strategy effects and already staged under generation methods and quality evaluation, but this pass reserved prompt-only variation for the prompt-engineering node unless it bridges to model or workflow comparisons.

screening_decisions.title: Generative Students: Using LLM-Simulated Student Profiles to Support Question Item Evaluation
screening_decisions.reason: Important profile-based generation evidence already covered in quality evaluation, but the staged baseline did not identify a model-family, post-training, or aligned workflow comparison strong enough for this node.

screening_decisions.title: Simulated Students in Tutoring Dialogues summary and mirror pages
screening_decisions.reason: Duplicate secondary summaries of the arXiv/author PDF source already included as candidate-scarlatos-2026.

screening_summary: Targeted bootstrap screening included five sources with direct evidence on model/version sensitivity, model-prompt specificity, prompt-based versus post-trained dialogue simulators, multi-model LLM respondent ensembles, and history/profile/RL workflow effects. Prompt-only sources were treated as boundary evidence for the adjacent prompt-engineering node.
limitations: This was a targeted bootstrap pass, not a systematic review.

Included studies span learner-response prediction, multiple-choice item response, College Algebra item evaluation, NAEP ability simulation, and tutoring dialogue; model-effect claims must remain task- and metric-bound.
The Duan et al. source was submitted to arXiv on 2026-05-28 and should be treated as current preprint evidence, not peer-reviewed consensus.
The pass prioritized sources with accessible primary full text and may miss non-indexed or paywalled studies.
